highlights.description: Agentic AI threat modeling with agent capabilities and execution context
Credential flow tracking for token exfiltration and replay attack modeling
WebSocket security configuration and CSWSH modeling
LLM-as-Judge evaluation package with structured-evaluation integration

added.description: `AgentCapabilities` type for AI agent tool access and autonomy levels
`ExecutionContext` type for runtime environment and privilege modeling
`CredentialFlow` type for token lifecycle tracking
`AttackPattern` type for reusable attack pattern templates
`WebSocketConfig` type for WebSocket endpoint security
Trust modeling fields: `ImplicitlyTrusted`, `TrustAssumption`, `TrustViolationRisk` on Boundary
Flow security fields: `Protocol`, `Encrypted`, `Authenticated`, `CrossOrigin`
New boundary types: `container`, `sandbox`, `agent`, `origin`
New flow types: `credential`, `websocket`, `cswsh`, `lateral`
`evaluation` package for LLM-as-Judge workflows
Embedded rubrics: `VulnerabilityArticleRubric()`, `ThreatModelRubric()`, `DiagramRubric()`
`ToEvaluationReport()` and `ToClaimsReport()` conversion methods
Vulnerability article template for security advisory evaluation

fixed.description: Windows compatibility for embedded rubric loading (`path.Join` for `embed.FS`)

`NetworkInfo` and `CloudInfo` for network topology mapping

`ThreatStatusTheoretical` for threats derived from STRIDE/LINDDUN

Attack tree diagram type with AND/OR logic gates (`attack-tree`)

Response actions for incident response workflows

CVE references in mappings (`CVEMapping` type)
D2 legend rendering for LINDDUN threats and mitigation status
